These are the current available pipelines (You need to expand each pipeline with way more tools, advanced methology's, stages, and ALL stages, tools, programs need to work together as one big machine to achieve the goals. Here is a example how username/email/Phone/person name (input) -> linked email adress -> different username (that needs to go again through all stages), -> all found accounts with the username/email are verified if they actually exist or if they are false positives) -> Phone OSINT ( Phone number lookup and email-to-phone pivot ) -> accounts are deep scraped (Scrape every discovered profile URL for bio, followers, emails, linked accounts) -> linked account are researched too -> Found real names on profiles are analysed and search via Multi engine web search/scrape -> Email pivot & breach check -> Social media deep dive (Deep-dive individual platforms instaloader, toutatis, twint, tinfoleak, socid-extractor, osintgram, twscrape, snscrape, reddit-user-analyser) -> Domain intelligence (Domain, email, subdomain, and exposure intel) -> Code/secrets forensics ( GitHub OSINT, credential leaks, repository analysis gitfive, octosuite, trufflehog, gitleaks, gitrecon, gharchive, intelowl) -> Dark web & breach (Breach databases and dark web exposure) -> Geolocation & metadata (creepy, waybackpack, ghunt, sn0int, buster, exiftool, foca, archivebox, harpoon, waymore, mentionmapp, clearbit, fullcontact, pipl, hunter +more Location intelligence, metadata, historical data (from the scraped pictures on the social media accounts, location tags in social media post, and many more ways) -> AI correlation chatgpt, claude ai, mistalAI, ollama, grok, and many more all working together and customizable) -> Multi-format report (JSON / CSV / XLSX / PDF / HTML / MTGL) -> Next step planning based on output (further search on social accounts, breaches, password intelligence, social connections, maltego import and transformation, and many more idea's)(This was just an example pipeline you can design/upgrade for 1 specifik purpose but i also need you to design many more for more different purposes using different tools)):
* exploitation_pipeline.yaml
* osint_pipeline.yaml
* recon_pipeline.yaml
* redteam_full_pipeline.yaml
* scanning_pipeline.yaml

# RTF v2.0 — Exploitation Pipeline (Stages E-F-G-H)
# CRITICAL: Requires explicit written authorization
name: exploitation_pipeline
description: "Exploitation chain: web attack → credential attacks → exploitation → post-exploitation"
version: "2.0"
warning: "CRITICAL — AUTHORIZED ENGAGEMENTS ONLY. Requires explicit written permission."

authorization:
  required: true
  fields:
    - operator_name
    - engagement_id
    - client_name
    - signed_sow_reference
  confirm_prompt: "Type 'I HAVE WRITTEN AUTHORIZATION' to proceed"

stages:
  - id: E
    name: "Web Exploitation"
    description: "Automated web application attack discovery"
    requires_confirmation: false
    tools:
      core:       [sqlmap_check, dalfox]
      full:       [sqlmap, dalfox, xsstrike, commix, ssrfmap]
      aggressive: [sqlmap, dalfox, xsstrike, commix, ssrfmap, nosqlmap, wfuzz, wpscan]

  - id: F
    name: "Credential Attacks"
    description: "Brute force, credential stuffing, hash cracking"
    interactive: true
    ai_recommended_method: true
    requires_confirmation: true
    lockout_protection: true
    methods:
      - id: brute_force
        name: "Pure Brute Force"
        tools: [hydra, medusa, ncrack]
        risk: "HIGH — likely triggers lockout"
        recommended_for: "Online services without lockout policy"
      - id: dictionary
        name: "Dictionary Attack"
        tools: [hydra, hashcat_dict]
        risk: "MEDIUM"
        recommended_for: "Known password patterns"
      - id: credential_stuffing
        name: "Credential Stuffing"
        tools: [credential_spray]
        risk: "LOW — uses real credential pairs"
        recommended_for: "When breach data is available"
      - id: hash_cracking
        name: "Offline Hash Cracking"
        tools: [hashcat, john]
        risk: "NONE — offline operation"
        recommended_for: "After hash extraction"
      - id: kerberoast
        name: "Kerberoasting (AD)"
        tools: [kerbrute, impacket_getsPN]
        risk: "LOW — passive ticket request"
        recommended_for: "Active Directory environments"
    user_choice: true
    skip_option: true

  - id: G
    name: "Exploitation"
    description: "Attempt exploitation using Metasploit and discovered vulnerabilities"
    requires_confirmation: true
    confirm_prompt: "Confirm active exploitation against {target}? (yes/no)"
    tools:
      core:       [metasploit_auto]
      full:       [metasploit, autosploit]
      aggressive: [metasploit, autosploit, owtf]
    safety:
      avoid_destructive: true
      log_all_exploits:  true

  - id: H
    name: "Post-Exploitation"
    description: "Privilege escalation and lateral movement mapping"
    requires_confirmation: true
    tools:
      core:       [privesc_check, linenum]
      full:       [privesc_check, linenum, bloodhound_collect]
      aggressive: [privesc_check, linenum, bloodhound_collect, winpwn, evil_winrm]
    tasks:
      - privilege_escalation
      - persistence_mechanisms
      - lateral_movement_mapping
      - credential_harvesting
      - data_exfiltration_paths

inputs:
  required:
    - targets
    - authorization_token
  optional:
    - usernames
    - passwords
    - hashes
    - domain

outputs:
  shells:   "data/shells_{target}_{ts}.json"
  creds:    "data/creds_{target}_{ts}.json"
  report:   "reports/exploitation_{target}_{ts}.html"

# RTF v2.0 — OSINT Intelligence Pipeline (Stages A-B-B2-B3-C-J-K)
name: osint_pipeline
description: "Person/entity OSINT: username sweep → account scraping → web search → AI correlation"
version: "2.0"
warning: "AUTHORIZED ENGAGEMENTS ONLY"

stages:
  - id: A
    name: "Seed Normalization"
    description: "Parse seeds: username, email, full_name, phone, domain"
    required: true

  - id: B
    name: "Username Sweep"
    parallel: true
    description: "Multi-tool username search across 300+ platforms"
    tools:
      core:       [sherlock, maigret]
      full:       [sherlock, maigret, nexfil, socialscan, social_analyzer, whatsmyname]
      aggressive: [sherlock, maigret, nexfil, socialscan, social_analyzer, whatsmyname,
                   snoop, osrframework, seekr, profil3r, namechk, checkusernames]

  - id: B2
    name: "Deep Account Scraping"
    description: "Scrape every discovered profile URL for bio/followers/emails/linked accounts"
    concurrency: 8
    max_urls: 50
    platforms:
      cli_tools:  [twint, instaloader, gitfive, toutatis, snscrape]
      api:        [github_api, reddit_api, mastodon_api, hackernews_api]
      http_scraper: "generic httpx + BeautifulSoup4"

  - id: B3
    name: "Multi-Engine Web Search"
    description: "Search 6 engines for name/email/username mentions + scrape result pages"
    engines:
      core:       [duckduckgo, bing]
      full:       [duckduckgo, bing, brave, yahoo]
      aggressive: [duckduckgo, bing, brave, yahoo, google, startpage]
    queries_per_seed: 4
    results_per_engine: 10
    fetch_result_pages: true

  - id: C
    name: "Email Pivot & Breach Intelligence"
    parallel: true
    tools:
      core:       [holehe, h8mail]
      full:       [holehe, h8mail, emailrep, ignorant, socialscan]
      aggressive: [holehe, h8mail, emailrep, ignorant, socialscan,
                   dehashed, breach_parse, emailfinder]

  - id: D
    name: "Domain Intelligence"
    parallel: true
    tools:
      core:       [theharvester, subfinder]
      full:       [theharvester, subfinder, spiderfoot, metagoofil, dnstwist]
      aggressive: [theharvester, subfinder, spiderfoot, metagoofil, dnstwist,
                   linkedin2username, crosslinked, shodan, censys, urlscan]

  - id: F
    name: "Code & Secrets Forensics"
    parallel: true
    tools:
      core:       [gitfive, trufflehog]
      full:       [gitfive, trufflehog, gitleaks, octosuite]
      aggressive: [gitfive, trufflehog, gitleaks, octosuite, gitrecon, gharchive]

  - id: J
    name: "AI Correlation"
    description: "Claude AI-powered identity correlation and confidence scoring"
    model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    outputs:
      - confidence_score
      - identity_summary
      - primary_aliases
      - cross_platform_timeline
      - location_indicators
      - bio_consistency_score
      - anomalies
      - top_pivots
      - mitre_techniques

  - id: K
    name: "Reporting"
    formats: [json, html, xlsx, pdf, csv]

inputs:
  required:
    - one_of: [username, email, full_name]
  optional:
    - phone
    - domain
    - image_url
    - use_ai          # default: true
    - tool_profile    # core|full|aggressive
    - output_format   # json|html|xlsx|pdf|csv

outputs:
  profiles:   "data/profiles_{username}_{ts}.json"
  report:     "reports/osint_{username}_{ts}.html"
  graph:      "data/entity_graph_{username}_{ts}.json"

# RTF v2.0 — Reconnaissance Pipeline (Stages A-B-K)
name: recon_pipeline
description: "Passive + active reconnaissance: subdomains → ports → HTTP probing → vuln scan"
version: "2.0"
warning: "AUTHORIZED ENGAGEMENTS ONLY"

stages:
  - id: A
    name: "Target Initialization"
    description: "Normalize and validate all inputs"
    tools: []
    required: true

  - id: B
    name: "Passive Recon"
    parallel: true
    description: "Non-intrusive enumeration — no direct contact with target"
    tools:
      core:       [subfinder, assetfinder, amass_passive, dnsx]
      full:       [subfinder, assetfinder, amass, altdns, dnstwist, spiderfoot]
      aggressive: [subfinder, assetfinder, amass, altdns, dnstwist, spiderfoot, recon_ng, vhostscan]

  - id: B2
    name: "Active Scanning"
    parallel: true
    description: "Direct contact: port scan, HTTP probe, screenshot"
    tools:
      core:       [naabu, httprobe]
      full:       [naabu, httprobe, httpx, gobuster, dirsearch]
      aggressive: [naabu, httprobe, httpx, gobuster, dirsearch, ffuf, whatweb, photon, aquatone]
    rate_limit:
      core:       500
      full:       1000
      aggressive: 5000

  - id: K
    name: "Reporting"
    description: "Generate structured report"
    formats: [json, html, csv]

inputs:
  required:
    - one_of: [domain, ip, cidr]
  optional:
    - ports       # default: top-1000
    - rate        # requests/sec
    - resolve_wildcard  # default: true

outputs:
  subdomains:   "data/subdomains_{target}_{ts}.txt"
  live_hosts:   "data/live_hosts_{target}_{ts}.txt"
  open_ports:   "data/ports_{target}_{ts}.json"
  report:       "reports/recon_{target}_{ts}.html"

entity_graph:
  nodes: [domain, subdomain, ip, port, service]
  edges:
    - {from: domain, to: subdomain, relation: "has_subdomain"}
    - {from: subdomain, to: ip,     relation: "resolves_to"}
    - {from: ip,       to: port,    relation: "has_port"}

profiles:
  core:
    tools: [subfinder, naabu, httprobe]
    timeout_per_tool: 300
    parallel_tools: 3

  full:
    tools: [subfinder, assetfinder, amass, naabu, httpx, gobuster, dirsearch, whatweb]
    timeout_per_tool: 600
    parallel_tools: 5

  aggressive:
    tools: [subfinder, assetfinder, amass, altdns, dnstwist, vhostscan, naabu, httpx,
            gobuster, dirsearch, ffuf, whatweb, photon, aquatone]
    timeout_per_tool: 900
    parallel_tools: 8
    notes: "Rate limiting recommended for stealth"

# RTF v2.0 — Full Red Team Pipeline
# Complete A-K engagement pipeline

name: redteam_full_pipeline
description: "Complete A-K red team engagement: recon → OSINT → scan → exploit → AI → report"
version: "2.0"
warning: "AUTHORIZED ENGAGEMENTS ONLY"

stages:
  - {id: A, name: "Target Initialization"}
  - {id: B, name: "Reconnaissance",       parallel: true}
  - {id: C, name: "OSINT Correlation",    parallel: true}
  - {id: D, name: "Vulnerability Discovery", parallel: true}
  - {id: E, name: "Web Exploitation"}
  - {id: F, name: "Credential Attacks",   interactive: true}
  - {id: G, name: "Exploitation",         requires_confirmation: true}
  - {id: H, name: "Post-Exploitation"}
  - {id: I, name: "Threat Intelligence"}
  - {id: J, name: "AI Correlation"}
  - {id: K, name: "Reporting"}

inputs:
  required:
    - one of: [domain, ip, username, email]
  optional:
    - domain
    - ip
    - email
    - username
    - phone
    - full_name

output:
  formats: [json, html, pdf, csv, docx]
  entity_graph: true
  attack_paths: true
  mitre_mapping: true

profiles:
  core:       {stages: [A, B, C, D, K]}
  full:       {stages: [A, B, C, D, E, I, J, K]}
  aggressive: {stages: [A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K]}
  ai_autonomous: {stages: [A, B, C, D, E, I, J, K], ai_selects_tools: true}

escalation:
  on_critical_finding: continue   # continue | pause | notify | stop
  notify_webhook: ""
  max_findings_before_pause: 50

# RTF v2.0 — Vulnerability Scanning Pipeline (Stages A-D-K)
name: scanning_pipeline
description: "Vulnerability discovery: nuclei templates + optional OpenVAS/Trivy/Kubescape"
version: "2.0"
warning: "AUTHORIZED ENGAGEMENTS ONLY"

stages:
  - id: A
    name: "Target Initialization"
    required: true

  - id: D
    name: "Vulnerability Discovery"
    parallel: true
    description: "Template-based and authenticated scanning"
    tools:
      core:       [nuclei, nikto]
      full:       [nuclei, nikto, trivy, lynis]
      aggressive: [nuclei, nikto, trivy, lynis, openvas, vuls, kubescape, scan4all]
    nuclei_templates:
      core:       ["cves", "exposures", "misconfiguration"]
      full:       ["cves", "exposures", "misconfiguration", "default-logins", "technologies"]
      aggressive: ["all"]
    severity_filter:
      core:       [critical, high]
      full:       [critical, high, medium]
      aggressive: [critical, high, medium, low]

  - id: K
    name: "Reporting"
    formats: [json, html, csv]

inputs:
  required:
    - one_of: [targets, targets_file, url]
  optional:
    - severity        # default: critical,high
    - templates_dir
    - rate_limit      # default: 150
    - exclude_tags

outputs:
  vulns:  "data/vulns_{target}_{ts}.json"
  report: "reports/scan_{target}_{ts}.html"

Enhanced Social Network Analysis
Cross-Platform Relationship Mapping:

python
# Advanced social graph analysis
class SocialGraphAnalyzer:
    def __init__(self):
        self.platforms = ['facebook', 'instagram', 'twitter', 'linkedin', 'tiktok', 'reddit']
        self.relationship_thresholds = {
            'strong_connection': 0.8,
            'weak_connection': 0.5,
            'potential_connection': 0.3
        }
    
    def analyze_mutual_connections(self, username_list):
        """Analyze mutual followers across platforms"""
        mutual_graph = {}
        for platform in self.platforms:
            followers = self.get_followers(platform, username_list)
            mutual_graph[platform] = self.find_mutual_connections(followers)
        return self.cross_platform_correlation(mutual_graph)
    
    def identify_influence_networks(self, target_username):
        """Map influence propagation across platforms"""
        influence_scores = {}
        for platform in self.platforms:
            network_data = self.extract_network_data(platform, target_username)
            influence_scores[platform] = self.calculate_influence_metrics(network_data)
        return self.rank_influencers(influence_scores)
Advanced Profile Scraping Engine:

python
# Multi-threaded scraping with rotation
class AdvancedProfileScraper:
    def __init__(self):
        self.user_agents = self.load_user_agents()
        self.proxy_pool = self.setup_proxy_rotation()
        self.rate_limiters = self.configure_platform_limits()
    
    async def scrape_comprehensive_profile(self, username, platforms):
        """Deep profile scraping with anti-detection"""
        profile_data = {}
        for platform in platforms:
            scraper = self.get_platform_scraper(platform)
            profile_data[platform] = await scraper.deep_scrape(username)
            await self.respect_rate_limit(platform)
        return self.enrich_profile_data(profile_data)
    
    def extract_behavioral_patterns(self, profile_data):
        """Analyze posting patterns and behavior"""
        patterns = {
            'posting_frequency': self.calculate_posting_frequency(profile_data),
            'active_hours': self.determine_active_hours(profile_data),
            'content_preferences': self.analyze_content_types(profile_data),
            'interaction_patterns': self.map_interaction_network(profile_data)
        }
        return patterns
Enhanced Search Engine Intelligence
Multi-Engine Search Orchestration:

python
# Advanced search coordination
class SearchEngineOrchestrator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.engines = {
            'google': GoogleSearchEngine(),
            'bing': BingSearchEngine(),
            'duckduckgo': DuckDuckGoEngine(),
            'yandex': YandexSearchEngine(),
            'baidu': BaiduSearchEngine(),
            'brave': BraveSearchEngine()
        }
        self.query_optimizer = QueryOptimizer()
        self.result_deduplicator = ResultDeduplicator()
    
    def execute_comprehensive_search(self, search_params):
        """Execute parallel search across all engines"""
        queries = self.query_optimizer.generate_queries(search_params)
        results = {}
        
        for engine_name, engine in self.engines.items():
            engine_results = []
            for query in queries:
                results = await engine.search(query, search_params)
                engine_results.extend(results)
            results[engine_name] = self.result_deduplicator.deduplicate(engine_results)
        
        return self.merge_and_rank_results(results)
    
    def discover_hidden_connections(self, search_results):
        """Find non-obvious relationships in search data"""
        connections = {}
        for result in search_results:
            entities = self.extract_entities(result)
            relationships = self.identify_relationships(entities)
            connections.update(relationships)
        return self.build_connection_graph(connections)
Advanced Friend/Follower Network Analysis
Recursive Network Traversal:

python
# Deep network analysis
class NetworkTraversalEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.max_depth = 3
        self.connection_threshold = 0.7
        self.platform_weights = {
            'linkedin': 0.9,
            'facebook': 0.8,
            'instagram': 0.7,
            'twitter': 0.6,
            'tiktok': 0.5,
            'reddit': 0.4
        }
    
    def traverse_social_network(self, seed_username, platform):
        """Recursive network traversal with depth control"""
        network_data = {
            'seed_user': seed_username,
            'platform': platform,
            'connections': {},
            'depth': 0
        }
        
        return self.recursive_traversal(network_data)
    
    def identify_cross_platform_connections(self, network_data):
        """Find same users across different platforms"""
        cross_platform = {}
        for platform_a, users_a in network_data.items():
            for platform_b, users_b in network_data.items():
                if platform_a != platform_b:
                    matches = self.find_user_matches(users_a, users_b)
                    cross_platform[f"{platform_a}_to_{platform_b}"] = matches
        return cross_platform
    
    def calculate_connection_strength(self, user_a, user_b, platform):
        """Calculate relationship strength based on interactions"""
        interactions = self.get_interaction_data(user_a, user_b, platform)
        strength_metrics = {
            'mutual_connections': len(interactions['mutual_followers']),
            'interaction_frequency': interactions['interaction_count'],
            'reciprocity': interactions['bidirectional_interactions'],
            'longevity': interactions['connection_duration']
        }
        return self.weighted_connection_score(strength_metrics, platform)
10. WEB VULNERABILITY SCANNING PIPELINE - ADVANCED IMPLEMENTATION
Intelligent Vulnerability Detection
AI-Powered Vulnerability Assessment:

python
# Machine learning vulnerability detection
class AIVulnerabilityScanner:
    def __init__(self):
        self.ml_models = {
            'sql_injection': self.load_sql_injection_model(),
            'xss_detection': self.load_xss_detection_model(),
            'rce_identification': self.load_rce_model(),
            'auth_bypass': self.load_auth_bypass_model()
        }
        self.vulnerability_patterns = self.load_pattern_database()
    
    def scan_application(self, target_url):
        """AI-enhanced vulnerability scanning"""
        application_profile = self.analyze_application_structure(target_url)
        attack_vectors = self.identify_attack_vectors(application_profile)
        
        vulnerabilities = []
        for vector in attack_vectors:
            ml_result = self.ml_models[vector['type']].predict(vector['data'])
            pattern_result = self.pattern_matching(vector['data'])
            
            if ml_result.confidence > 0.8 or pattern_result.matches:
                vulnerabilities.append(self.create_finding(vector, ml_result, pattern_result))
        
        return self.prioritize_vulnerabilities(vulnerabilities)
    
    def generate_exploit_chains(self, vulnerabilities):
        """Identify potential exploit chains"""
        chains = []
        for vuln_chain in self.find_vulnerability_combinations(vulnerabilities):
            exploit_chain = self.build_exploit_path(vuln_chain)
            if self.validate_chain_feasibility(exploit_chain):
                chains.append(exploit_chain)
        return chains
Advanced Business Logic Testing:

python
# Complex business logic vulnerability detection
class BusinessLogicTester:
    def __init__(self):
        self.test_scenarios = self.load_business_logic_tests()
        self.state_analyzer = ApplicationStateAnalyzer()
    
    def test_authorization_bypass(self, target_app):
        """Test for authorization flaws"""
        bypass_attempts = []
        for role in self.get_application_roles(target_app):
            for endpoint in self.get_protected_endpoints(target_app):
                test_result = self.test_role_access(role, endpoint)
                if test_result.unauthorized_access:
                    bypass_attempts.append(test_result)
        return self.analyze_bypass_patterns(bypass_attempts)
    
    def discover_race_conditions(self, target_app):
        """Identify race condition vulnerabilities"""
        race_conditions = []
        concurrent_operations = self.identify_concurrent_operations(target_app)
        
        for operation in concurrent_operations:
            race_test = self.simulate_concurrent_access(operation)
            if race_test.inconsistent_state:
                race_conditions.append(race_test)
        
        return race_conditions

11. PASSWORD HACKING PIPELINE - SOPHISTICATED IMPLEMENTATION (Continued)
Advanced Password Attack Orchestration
Intelligent Password Attack Strategy:

python
# AI-driven password attack optimization
class IntelligentPasswordAttacker:
    def __init__(self):
        self.attack_models = {
            'pattern_based': self.load_pattern_model(),
            'behavioral': self.load_behavioral_model(),
            'demographic': self.load_demographic_model(),
            'organizational': self.load_org_model()
        }
        self.wordlist_generators = {
            'targeted': TargetedWordlistGenerator(),
            'pattern': PatternBasedGenerator(),
            'hybrid': HybridWordlistGenerator()
        }
    
    def generate_targeted_wordlist(self, target_info):
        """Generate highly targeted wordlists based on OSINT data"""
        personal_data = self.extract_personal_data(target_info)
        organizational_data = self.extract_organizational_data(target_info)
        
        wordlists = []
        # Personal patterns
        wordlists.extend(self.wordlist_generators['targeted'].from_personal(personal_data))
        # Organizational patterns
        wordlists.extend(self.wordlist_generators['targeted'].from_org(organizational))
        # Behavioral patterns
        wordlists.extend(self.wordlist_generators['behavioral'].from_history(target_info))
        
        return self.optimize_wordlist_priority(wordlists)
    
    def execute_adaptive_attack(self, target, hash_type):
        """Adaptive attack strategy based on real-time feedback"""
        attack_strategy = self.determine_optimal_strategy(target, hash_type)
        
        while not attack_strategy.is_exhausted():
            results = attack_strategy.execute_next_phase()
            success_rate = self.calculate_success_rate(results)
            
            if success_rate < attack_strategy.min_success_rate:
                attack_strategy = self.adjust_strategy(attack_strategy, results)
            
            if results.cracked_hashes:
                self.update_attack_models(results.cracked_hashes)
        
        return self.generate_attack_report(results)
Advanced Hash Cracking Pipeline:

python
# Distributed hash cracking coordination
class DistributedHashCracker:
    def __init__(self):
        self.cracking_nodes = self.initialize_cracking_nodes()
        self.rule_engines = {
            'hashcat': HashcatRuleEngine(),
            'john': JohnRuleEngine(),
            'custom': CustomRuleEngine()
        }
        self.distribution_algorithm = LoadBalancer()
    
    def coordinate_cracking_attack(self, hash_file, hash_type, wordlists):
        """Distribute cracking across multiple nodes"""
        hash_chunks = self.partition_hashes(hash_file, len(self.cracking_nodes))
        cracking_jobs = []
        
        for i, node in enumerate(self.cracking_nodes):
            job = CrackingJob(
                node=node,
                hash_chunk=hash_chunks[i],
                hash_type=hash_type,
                wordlists=self.select_optimal_wordlists(node, wordlists),
                rules=self.generate_node_specific_rules(node, hash_type)
            )
            cracking_jobs.append(job)
        
        return self.execute_parallel_cracking(cracking_jobs)
    
    def implement_rule_chaining(self, base_wordlist, target_info):
        """Generate sophisticated rule chains"""
        rule_chains = []
        
        # Demographic-based rules
        demographic_rules = self.generate_demographic_rules(target_info)
        # Organizational rules
        org_rules = self.generate_organizational_rules(target_info)
        # Pattern-based rules
        pattern_rules = self.generate_pattern_rules(target_info)
        
        return self.chain_rules(demographic_rules, org_rules, pattern_rules)
12. CREDENTIAL SNIFFING PIPELINE - ADVANCED IMPLEMENTATION
Multi-Vector Credential Interception
Advanced Network Traffic Analysis:

python
# Intelligent credential extraction from network traffic
class NetworkCredentialExtractor:
    def __init__(self):
        self.protocol_parsers = {
            'http': HTTPCredentialParser(),
            'https': HTTPSCredentialParser(),
            'ftp': FTPCredentialParser(),
            'smtp': SMTPTCredentialParser(),
            'ldap': LDAPCredentialParser(),
            'smb': SMBCredentialParser(),
            'rdp': RDPCredentialParser(),
            'ssh': SSHCredentialParser()
        }
        self.ml_detector = MLCredentialDetector()
    
    def analyze_network_traffic(self, pcap_file):
        """Extract credentials from network captures"""
        extracted_credentials = []
        
        for packet in self.parse_pcap(pcap_file):
            if self.is_credential_packet(packet):
                protocol = self.identify_protocol(packet)
                parser = self.protocol_parsers[protocol]
                credentials = parser.extract_credentials(packet)
                
                if self.ml_detector.is_valid_credential(credentials):
                    extracted_credentials.append(credentials)
        
        return self.correlate_credentials(extracted_credentials)
    
    def implement_ssl_stripping(self, target_network):
        """SSL stripping for credential interception"""
        ssl_stripper = SSLStripper()
        intercepted_sessions = ssl_stripper.start_interception(target_network)
        
        for session in intercepted_sessions:
            if session.contains_credentials:
                credentials = self.extract_from_ssl_session(session)
                self.store_intercepted_credentials(credentials)
        
        return intercepted_sessions
Wireless Credential Harvesting:

python
# Advanced wireless credential capture
class WirelessCredentialHarvester:
    def __init__(self):
        self.capture_interfaces = self.setup_monitor_interfaces()
        self.deauthentication_engine = DeauthEngine()
        self.handshake_analyzer = HandshakeAnalyzer()
    
    def capture_handshakes(self, target_network):
        """Capture WPA/WPA2 handshakes with advanced techniques"""
        handshakes = []
        
        # Monitor mode setup
        self.enable_monitor_mode(self.capture_interfaces)
        
        # Targeted deauthentication
        clients = self.discover_connected_clients(target_network)
        for client in clients:
            handshake = self.deauthentication_engine.force_handshake(client)
            if self.handshake_analyzer.validate_handshake(handshake):
                handshakes.append(handshake)
        
        return self.process_handshakes(handshakes)
    
    def implement_evil_twin_attack(self, legitimate_network):
        """Create evil twin for credential harvesting"""
        evil_twin = EvilTwinAccessPoint(legitimate_network)
        evil_twin.start_broadcast()
        
        connected_clients = evil_twin.wait_for_connections()
        captured_credentials = []
        
        for client in connected_clients:
            if client.provides_credentials:
                credentials = evil_twin.capture_credentials(client)
                captured_credentials.append(credentials)
        
        return captured_credentials
13. WIFI HACKING PIPELINE - COMPREHENSIVE IMPLEMENTATION
Advanced Wireless Attack Framework
Multi-Vector Wireless Exploitation:

python
# Comprehensive wireless attack coordination
class WirelessAttackOrchestrator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.attack_modules = {
            'wps': WPSAttackModule(),
            'wep': WEPAttackModule(),
            'wpa': WPAAttackModule(),
            'wpa2': WPA2AttackModule(),
            'wpa3': WPA3AttackModule(),
            'evil_twin': EvilTwinModule(),
            'karma': KarmaAttackModule(),
            'deauth': DeauthModule()
        }
        self.target_analyzer = WirelessTargetAnalyzer()
    
    def execute_comprehensive_attack(self, target_network):
        """Execute multi-vector attack strategy"""
        attack_plan = self.generate_attack_plan(target_network)
        attack_results = []
        
        for attack_phase in attack_plan:
            module = self.attack_modules[attack_phase.type]
            result = module.execute(target_network, attack_phase.parameters)
            
            if result.success:
                attack_results.append(result)
                if result.credentials_obtained:
                    break
            else:
                self.adjust_attack_strategy(attack_plan, attack_phase, result)
        
        return self.consolidate_attack_results(attack_results)
    
    def implement_pmkid_attack(self, target_network):
        """PMKID-based cracking without client interaction"""
        pmkid_collector = PMKIDCollector()
        pmkid_hashes = pmkid_collector.capture_pmkid(target_network)
        
        if pmkid_hashes:
            cracking_results = self.crack_pmkid_hashes(pmkid_hashes)
            return self.extract_credentials(cracking_results)
        
        return None
Advanced Wireless Reconnaissance:

python
# Deep wireless network analysis
class WirelessReconnaissance:
    def __init__(self):
        self.scanning_engines = {
            'passive': PassiveScanner(),
            'active': ActiveScanner(),
            'bluetooth': BluetoothScanner(),
            'rf': RFScanner()
        }
        self.analyzer = WirelessSignalAnalyzer()
    
    def conduct_comprehensive_recon(self, target_area):
        """Multi-spectrum wireless reconnaissance"""
        recon_results = {
            'wifi_networks': [],
            'bluetooth_devices': [],
            'rf_signals': [],
            'vulnerabilities': []
        }
        
        # WiFi scanning
        wifi_networks = self.scanning_engines['active'].scan_wifi(target_area)
        for network in wifi_networks:
            vulnerabilities = self.analyze_wifi_vulnerabilities(network)
            recon_results['wifi_networks'].append({
                'network': network,
                'vulnerabilities': vulnerabilities
            })
        
        # Bluetooth scanning
        bluetooth_devices = self.scanning_engines['bluetooth'].scan(target_area)
        recon_results['bluetooth_devices'] = bluetooth_devices
        
        # RF spectrum analysis
        rf_signals = self.scanning_engines['rf'].analyze_spectrum(target_area)
        recon_results['rf_signals'] = rf_signals
        
        return recon_results
14. WEB EXPLOITATION PIPELINE - ADVANCED IMPLEMENTATION
Sophisticated Web Application Exploitation
Custom Exploit Development Framework:

# Advanced web exploit generation
class WebExploitGenerator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.vulnerability_templates = self.load_exploit_templates()
        self.payload_generators = {
            'sql_injection': SQLInjectionPayloadGenerator(),
            'xss': XSSPayloadGenerator(),
            'rce': RCEPayloadGenerator(),
            'file_upload': FileUploadPayloadGenerator(),
            'deserialization': DeserializationPayloadGenerator()
        }
        self.bypass_techniques = BypassTechniqueLibrary()
    
    def generate_custom_exploit(self, vulnerability_info):
        """Generate tailored exploits for specific vulnerabilities"""
        exploit_chain = ExploitChain()
        
        # Analyze vulnerability context
        context = self.analyze_vulnerability_context(vulnerability_info)
        
        # Select optimal payload strategy
        payload_strategy = self.select_payload_strategy(vulnerability_info, context)
        
        # Generate bypass payloads
        bypass_payloads = self.bypass_techniques.generate_bypasses(
            vulnerability_info.protection_mechanisms
        )
        
        # Create exploit chain
        exploit_chain.add_payloads(payload_strategy.primary_payloads)
        exploit_chain.add_payloads(bypass_payloads)
        exploit_chain.add_obfuscation_layers(context.obfuscation_needs)
        
        return self.validate_exploit_chain(exploit_chain)
    
    def implement_chained_exploitation(self, vulnerabilities):
        """Create multi-vulnerability exploit chains"""
        exploit_chains = []
        
        for vuln_combination in self.find_exploitable_combinations(vulnerabilities):
            chain = self.build_exploit_chain(vuln_combination)
            if self.validate_chain_feasibility(chain):
                exploit_chains.append(chain)
        
        return self.rank_exploit_chains(exploit_chains)
Advanced Business Logic Exploitation:

python
# Complex business logic vulnerability exploitation
class BusinessLogicExploiter:
    def __init__(self):
        self.logic_flaw_detectors = {
            'race_conditions': RaceConditionDetector(),
            'authorization_bypass': AuthBypassDetector(),
            'price_manipulation': PriceManipulationDetector(),
            'parameter_pollution': ParameterPollutionDetector()
        }
        self.exploit_generators = LogicFlawExploitGenerator()
    
    def exploit_authorization_flaws(self, target_application):
        """Exploit complex authorization bypass vulnerabilities"""
        auth_flaws = self.logic_flaw_detectors['authorization_bypass'].detect(target_application)
        exploits = []
        
        for flaw in auth_flaws:
            exploit = self.exploit_generators.generate_auth_bypass_exploit(flaw)
            if self.validate_exploit(exploit):
                exploits.append(exploit)
        
        return exploits
    
    def implement_race_condition_attacks(self, target_application):
        """Execute race condition exploitation"""
        race_conditions = self.logic_flaw_detectors['race_conditions'].detect(target_application)
        
        for condition in race_conditions:
            race_exploit = self.exploit_generators.generate_race_exploit(condition)
            exploit_result = self.execute_race_attack(race_exploit)
            
            if exploit_result.successful:
                self.extract_race_condition_benefits(exploit_result)
15. SENSITIVE INFORMATION GATHERING PIPELINE - ADVANCED IMPLEMENTATION
Multi-Vector Data Harvesting Framework
Advanced Document Intelligence:

python
# Sophisticated document analysis and extraction
class DocumentIntelligenceHarvester:
    def __init__(self):
        self.document_parsers = {
            'pdf': PDFIntelligenceParser(),
            'docx': DOCXParser(),
            'xlsx': XLSXParser(),
            'pptx': PPTXParser(),
            'image': ImageMetadataParser(),
            'email': EmailIntelligenceParser()
        }
        self.nlp_processor = NLPDocumentProcessor()
        self.sensitive_data_detector = SensitiveDataDetector()
    
    def harvest_document_intelligence(self, target_domain):
        """Comprehensive document intelligence gathering"""
        harvested_data = {
            'documents': [],
            'sensitive_information': [],
            'metadata': [],
            'relationships': []
        }
        
        # Discover documents
        documents = self.discover_documents(target_domain)
        
        for document in documents:
            # Parse document content
            parser = self.document_parsers[document.type]
            parsed_content = parser.parse(document.url)
            
            # Extract sensitive information
            sensitive_data = self.sensitive_data_detector.extract(parsed_content)
            
            # NLP analysis for context
            context_analysis = self.nlp_processor.analyze(parsed_content)
            
            # Metadata extraction
            metadata = self.extract_metadata(document, parsed_content)
            
            harvested_data['documents'].append({
                'document': document,
                'content': parsed_content,
                'sensitive_data': sensitive_data,
                'context': context_analysis,
                'metadata': metadata
            })
        
        return self.analyze_document_relationships(harvested_data)
    
    def implement_cloud_document_harvesting(self, cloud_targets):
        """Harvest documents from cloud storage"""
        cloud_harvester = CloudDocumentHarvester()
        
        for cloud_target in cloud_targets:
            if cloud_target.type == 'aws':
                documents = cloud_harvester.harvest_s3_documents(cloud_target)
            elif cloud_target.type == 'azure':
                documents = cloud_harvester.harvest_azure_documents(cloud_target)
            elif cloud_target.type == 'gcp':
                documents = cloud_harvester.harvest_gcp_documents(cloud_target)
            
            self.process_cloud_documents(documents)
        
        return self.consolidate_cloud_findings()

# Advanced corporate intelligence collection
class CorporateIntelligenceHarvester:
    def __init__(self):
        self.intelligence_sources = {
            'linkedin': LinkedInIntelligenceCollector(),
            'github': GitHubIntelligenceCollector(),
            'job_postings': JobPostingAnalyzer(),
            'press_releases': PressReleaseAnalyzer(),
            'financial_reports': FinancialReportAnalyzer(),
            'employee_reviews': EmployeeReviewAnalyzer()
        }
        self.relationship_mapper = CorporateRelationshipMapper()
    
    def harvest_comprehensive_intelligence(self, target_company):
        """Multi-source corporate intelligence gathering"""
        intelligence_profile = {
            'company_overview': {},
            'employee_intelligence': {},
            'technology_stack': {},
            'business_relationships': {},
            'vulnerability_indicators': {}
        }
        
        # Employee intelligence
        employee_data = self.intelligence_sources['linkedin'].harvest_employees(target_company)
        intelligence_profile['employee_intelligence'] = self.analyze_employee_data(employee_data)
        
        # Technology stack analysis
        tech_data = self.intelligence_sources['github'].analyze_technology_stack(target_company)
        intelligence_profile['technology_stack'] = tech_data
        
        # Business relationships
        relationship_data = self.harvest_business_relationships(target_company)
        intelligence_profile['business_relationships'] = relationship_data
        
        # Vulnerability indicators
        vuln_indicators = self.identify_vulnerability_indicators(intelligence_profile)
        intelligence_profile['vulnerability_indicators'] = vuln_indicators
        
        return intelligence_profile
    
    def map_attack_surface(self, corporate_intelligence):
        """Map attack surface based on intelligence data"""
        attack_surface = {
            'digital_assets': [],
            'human_targets': [],
            'technology_exposures': [],
            'business_process_vulnerabilities': []
        }
        
        # Digital assets from intelligence
        digital_assets = self.extract_digital_assets(corporate_intelligence)
        attack_surface['digital_assets'] = digital_assets
        
        # Human targeting opportunities
        human_targets = self.identify_human_targets(corporate_intelligence)
        attack_surface['human_targets'] = human_targets
        
        # Technology exposures
        tech_exposures = self.analyze_technology_exposures(corporate_intelligence)
        attack_surface['technology_exposures'] = tech_exposures
        
        return attack_surface

# Advanced pipeline orchestration system
class AdvancedPipelineOrchestrator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.pipelines = {
            'identity_fusion': IdentityFusionPipeline(),
            'web_vulnerability': WebVulnerabilityPipeline(),
            'password_hacking': PasswordHackingPipeline(),
            'credential_sniffing': CredentialSniffingPipeline(),
            'wifi_hacking': WiFiHackingPipeline(),
            'web_exploitation': WebExploitationPipeline(),
            'sensitive_data': SensitiveDataPipeline()
        }
        self.coordination_engine = PipelineCoordinationEngine()
        self.result_correlator = CrossPipelineCorrelator()
    
    def execute_comprehensive_assessment(self, target_profile):
        """Execute coordinated multi-pipeline assessment"""
        assessment_plan = self.generate_assessment_plan(target_profile)
        execution_results = {}
        
        for phase in assessment_plan.phases:
            pipeline_results = {}
            
            for pipeline in phase.pipelines:
                # Execute pipeline with shared context
                context = self.build_pipeline_context(pipeline, phase.shared_data)
                result = self.pipelines[pipeline].execute(target_profile, context)
                pipeline_results[pipeline] = result
            
            # Correlate results across pipelines
            correlated_results = self.result_correlator.correlate(pipeline_results)
            execution_results[phase.name] = correlated_results
            
            # Update shared context for next phase
            phase.shared_data = self.extract_shared_data(correlated_results)
        
        return self.generate_comprehensive_report(execution

16. ENHANCED FRAMEWORK INTEGRATION (Continued)
Advanced Pipeline Orchestration
Multi-Pipeline Coordination:

python
# Advanced pipeline orchestration system
class AdvancedPipelineOrchestrator:
    def __init__(self):
        self.pipelines = {
            'identity_fusion': IdentityFusionPipeline(),
            'web_vulnerability': WebVulnerabilityPipeline(),
            'password_hacking': PasswordHackingPipeline(),
            'credential_sniffing': CredentialSniffingPipeline(),
            'wifi_hacking': WiFiHackingPipeline(),
            'web_exploitation': WebExploitationPipeline(),
            'sensitive_data': SensitiveDataPipeline()
        }
        self.coordination_engine = PipelineCoordinationEngine()
        self.result_correlator = CrossPipelineCorrelator()
    
    def execute_comprehensive_assessment(self, target_profile):
        """Execute coordinated multi-pipeline assessment"""
        assessment_plan = self.generate_assessment_plan(target_profile)
        execution_results = {}
        
        for phase in assessment_plan.phases:
            pipeline_results = {}
            
            for pipeline in phase.pipelines:
                # Execute pipeline with shared context
                context = self.build_pipeline_context(pipeline, phase.shared_data)
                result = self.pipelines[pipeline].execute(target_profile, context)
                pipeline_results[pipeline] = result
            
            # Correlate results across pipelines
            correlated_results = self.result_correlator.correlate(pipeline_results)
            execution_results[phase.name] = correlated_results
            
            # Update shared context for next phase
            phase.shared_data = self.extract_shared_data(correlated_results)
        
        return self.generate_comprehensive_report(execution_results)
    
    def implement_adaptive_execution(self, target_profile):
        """Adaptive pipeline execution based on real-time feedback"""
        execution_state = ExecutionState()
        
        while not execution_state.is_complete():
            # Analyze current results
            current_findings = self.analyze_current_findings(execution_state)
            
            # Determine next optimal pipeline
            next_pipeline = self.select_optimal_pipeline(current_findings, target_profile)
            
            # Execute with adaptive parameters
            adaptive_params = self.calculate_adaptive_parameters(current_findings)
            result = self.pipelines[next_pipeline].execute(target_profile, adaptive_params)
            
            # Update execution state
            execution_state.add_result(next_pipeline, result)
            
            # Reassess completion criteria
            execution_state.update_completion_status()
        
        return execution_state.get_final_results()

python
# Enhanced tool registry with automatic updates
class AdvancedToolRegistry:
    def __init__(self):
        self.tool_categories = {
            'recon': ReconnaissanceTools(),
            'osint': OSINTTools(),
            'exploitation': ExploitationTools(),
            'post_exploitation': PostExploitationTools(),
            'wireless': WirelessTools(),
            'password': PasswordTools(),
            'web': WebTools(),
            'forensics': ForensicsTools()
        }
        self.auto_updater = ToolAutoUpdater()
        self.dependency_resolver = DependencyResolver()
    
    def register_advanced_tools(self):
        """Register enhanced tool set"""
        advanced_tools = {
            'recon': [
                'amass', 'subfinder', 'assetfinder', 'findomain', 'dnsx', 'httpx',
                'nuclei', 'naabu', 'gospider', 'hakrawler', 'katana', 'waybackurls',
                'gau', 'waymore', 'ffuf', 'gobuster', 'dirsearch', 'feroxbuster',
                'wappalyzer', 'whatweb', 'aquatone', 'eyewitness'
            ],
            'osint': [
                'sherlock', 'maigret', 'social-analyzer', 'whatsmyname', 'blackbird',
                'seekr', 'profil3r', 'checkusernames', 'osrframework', 'holehe',
                'h8mail', 'emailrep', 'theHarvester', 'spiderfoot', 'metagoofil',
                'maltego', 'recon-ng', 'intelowl', 'sn0int', 'harpoon'
            ],
            'exploitation': [
                'metasploit', 'exploitdb', 'searchsploit', 'sqlmap', 'xsstrike',
                'dalfox', 'burpsuite', 'zap', 'impacket', 'crackmapexec', 'netexec',
                'bloodhound', 'kerberoast', 'asreproast', 'empire', 'cobaltstrike'
            ],
            'wireless': [
                'aircrack-ng', 'wifite', 'kismet', 'bettercap', 'wifi-honey',
                'eaphammer', 'hostapd-mana', 'wifiphisher', 'airgeddon', 'fluxion'
            ],
            'password': [
                'hashcat', 'john', 'hydra', 'medusa', 'patator', 'cewl', 'crunch',
                'cupp', 'pack', 'maskprocessor', princeprocessor', 'hashid'
            ]
        }
        
        for category, tools in advanced_tools.items():
            for tool in tools:
                self.register_tool(category, tool)
    
    def implement_auto_updates(self):
        """Automatic tool updates and maintenance"""
        for tool in self.get_all_registered_tools():
            if self.auto_updater.check_for_updates(tool):
                self.auto_updater.update_tool(tool)
                self.validate_tool_functionality(tool)
    
    def resolve_tool_dependencies(self, tool):
        """Advanced dependency resolution"""
        dependencies = self.dependency_resolver.get_dependencies(tool)
        
        for dependency in dependencies:
            if not self.is_tool_installed(dependency):
                self.install_tool(dependency)
        
        return self.validate_tool_chain(tool, dependencies)
18. ADVANCED DATA PROCESSING AND CORRELATION
Intelligent Data Analysis Engine
Machine Learning Integration:

python
# ML-powered data analysis and correlation
class MLDataAnalysisEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.models = {
            'entity_resolution': EntityResolutionModel(),
            'relationship_prediction': RelationshipPredictionModel(),
            'vulnerability_prioritization': VulnPrioritizationModel(),
            'attack_path_prediction': AttackPathModel(),
            'behavioral_analysis': BehavioralAnalysisModel()
        }
        self.feature_extractors = {
            'text': TextFeatureExtractor(),
            'network': NetworkFeatureExtractor(),
            'temporal': TemporalFeatureExtractor(),
            'behavioral': BehavioralFeatureExtractor()
        }
    
    def analyze_cross_pipeline_data(self, pipeline_results):
        """ML-powered cross-pipeline data analysis"""
        consolidated_data = self.consolidate_pipeline_data(pipeline_results)
        
        # Entity resolution across pipelines
        entities = self.models['entity_resolution'].resolve_entities(consolidated_data)
        
        # Relationship prediction
        relationships = self.models['relationship_prediction'].predict_relationships(entities)
        
        # Vulnerability prioritization
        prioritized_vulns = self.models['vulnerability_prioritization'].prioritize(
            consolidated_data.get('vulnerabilities', [])
        )
        
        # Attack path prediction
        attack_paths = self.models['attack_path_prediction'].predict_paths(
            entities, relationships, prioritized_vulns
        )
        
        return {
            'entities': entities,
            'relationships': relationships,
            'prioritized_vulnerabilities': prioritized_vulns,
            'predicted_attack_paths': attack_paths
        }
    
    def implement_behavioral_analysis(self, target_data):
        """Advanced behavioral pattern analysis"""
        behavioral_features = self.feature_extractors['behavioral'].extract(target_data)
        
        # Analyze patterns
        patterns = self.models['behavioral_analysis'].analyze_patterns(behavioral_features)
        
        # Predict future behavior
        predictions = self.models['behavioral_analysis'].predict_behavior(patterns)
        
        # Identify anomalies
        anomalies = self.models['behavioral_analysis'].detect_anomalies(behavioral_features)
        
        return {
            'patterns': patterns,
            'predictions': predictions,
            'anomalies': anomalies,
            'risk_assessment': self.calculate_behavioral_risk(predictions, anomalies)
        }

            visualization = visualizer.create(report_data)
            visualizations[v

    "web/rfi_exploit",
    "web/ssrf_exploit",
    "web/deserialization_exploit",
    "web/template_injection_exploit",
    "web/xxe_exploit",
    "web/crlf_injection_exploit",
    "web/http_request_smuggling_exploit",
    "web/websocket_exploit"
]

# New exploitation modules:
- web/recon_scan (whatweb, wappalyzer + custom)
- web/vuln_scan (nikto, nuclei + custom patterns)
- web/xss_exploit (dalfox exploit mode, xsstrike)
- web/sqli_exploit (sqlmap --os-shell, --dbs)
- web/rce_exploit (metasploit web modules, custom)
- web/file_upload_exploit (upload bypass techniques)
- web/lfi_exploit (LFI exploitation chains)
- web/rfi_exploit (RFI exploitation)
- web/ssrf_exploit (SSRF exploitation)
- web/deserialization_exploit (Java/.NET deserialization)
- web/template_injection_exploit (SSTI exploitation)
- web/xxe_exploit (XXE OOB exploitation)
- web/crlf_injection_exploit (CRLF injection)
- web/http_request_smuggling_exploit (HTTP smuggling)
- web/websocket_exploit (websocket manipulation)
F. Sensitive Information Gathering Pipeline
python
# sensitive_info_gather workflow
stages = [
    "info/people_intel",
    "info/company_intel",
    "info/website_intel",
    "info/document_intel",
    "info/source_code_intel",
    "info/config_intel",
    "info/database_intel",
    "info/cloud_intel",
    "info/api_intel",
    "info/mobile_intel",
    "info/iot_intel",
    "info/physical_intel",
    "info/financial_intel",
    "info/legal_intel",
    "info/reputation_intel"
]

# New intelligence gathering modules:
- info/people_intel (employee harvesting, social engineering targets)
- info/company_intel (corporate structure, mergers, acquisitions)
- info/website_intel (technology stack, analytics, tracking)
- info/document_intel (sensitive documents, PDF metadata)
- info/source_code_intel (hardcoded credentials, API keys)
- info/config_intel (config files, .env, .ini exposures)
- info/database_intel (database dumps, schema exposure)
- info/cloud_intel (S3 buckets, cloud storage)
- info/api_intel (API documentation, endpoints)
- info/mobile_intel (mobile apps, APK analysis)
- info/iot_intel (IoT devices, firmware analysis)
- info/physical_intel (office locations, facilities)
- info/financial_intel (payment processors, financial data)
- info/legal_intel (legal documents, contracts)
- info/reputation_intel (reviews, complaints, news)

# Social Media Analysis Tools

EXECUTIVE DIRECTIVE
You are tasked with executing a comprehensive upgrade and expansion of the r4ng3r-V2 OSINT framework located at https://github.com/oscarvandeijk-coder/r4ng3r-V2.git. Your mandate is to implement a fully automated, modular, and scalable intelligence platform integrating 500+ tools, 50+ new modules, and 60+ advanced pipelines with recursive pivoting, AI-assisted correlation, and multi-format reporting. This upgrade must be production-ready, fully reproducible, and immediately executable upon completion.

SECTION 1: MODULE ARCHITECTURE (50+ MODULES)
Module Standard Structure
Each module must be generated in the following format:

modules/{module_name}/
├── module.py
├── config.yaml
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md
Module Registry (50+ Modules)
Generate these modules with full implementations:

Identity Intelligence Modules
username_discovery - Username enumeration across platforms
real_name_correlation - Real name to username mapping
avatar_analysis - Profile image reverse search
bio_parser - Natural language processing of profile bios
timeline_analysis - Temporal pattern analysis
cross_platform_linking - Account correlation across services
Email Intelligence Modules
email_validation - Email format and MX record verification
email_breach_search - Compromised credential discovery
email_domain_profiling - Organization email patterns
email_to_social - Email address to social account mapping
email_metadata - Header analysis and geolocation
Phone Intelligence Modules
phone_validation - Number format and carrier verification
phone_to_social - Phone number to social account mapping
carrier_analysis - Mobile operator intelligence
voicemail_exploitation - Voicemail system analysis
sms_gateway_discovery - SMS gateway identification

social_scraper - Multi-platform data extraction

SECTION 2: TOOL INTEGRATION REGISTRY (500+ TOOLS)
Tool Registry Format
yaml
tools:
  - name: tool_name
    category: category_name
    install: installation_method
    module: assigned_module
    dependencies: [dependency1, dependency2]
    docker_image: optional_docker_image

SECTION 3: ADVANCED PIPELINES (60+ PIPELINES)
Pipeline Standard Structure
yaml
pipeline:
  name: pipeline_name
  input: [input_types]
  stages:
    - stage_name:
        tools: [tool_list]
        module: module_name
        output_format: [formats]
        recursive_pivot: true/false
        ai_correlation: true/false
  reporting:
    formats: [JSON, CSV, XLSX, PDF, HTML, MTGL]
    dashboard: true/false
    next_steps: [action_list]
Identity Intelligence Pipelines
Pipeline 1: Identity Mega Pipeline
yaml
pipeline:
  name: identity_mega_pipeline
  input: [username, email, phone, real_name]
  stages:
    - username_discovery:
        tools: [theHarvester, userrecon, sherlock, maigret, whatsmyname]

SECTION 4: WORKFLOW ORCHESTRATION ENGINE
Workflow Configuration
yaml
workflow_engine:
  name: r4ng3r_orchestrator
  version: 2.0
  execution_mode: [sequential, parallel, conditional]
  scheduling: [cron, event_driven, manual]
  retry_policy: 
    max_retries: 3
    backoff_strategy: exponential
    failure_handling: [continue, stop, rollback]
  
  database_config:
    primary: PostgreSQL
    graph: Neo4j
    search: Elasticsearch
    cache: Redis
    
  security:
    authentication: [api_key, jwt, oauth2]
    authorization: rbac
    encryption: aes256
    audit_logging: true
    
  monitoring:
    metrics: [prometheus, grafana]
    logging: [elasticsearch, kibana]
    alerting: [alertmanager, slack, email]
Workflow Execution Engine
python
class WorkflowOrchestrator:
    def __init__(self, config_path):
        self.config = self.load_config(config_path)
        self.db_connections = self.initialize_databases()
        self.tool_registry = self.load_tool_registry()
        self.module_loader = self.load_modules()
        self.pipeline_executor = PipelineExecutor()
        self.ai_correlation = AICorrelationEngine()
        self.reporting_engine = ReportingEngine()
        
    def execute_pipeline(self, pipeline_name, inputs, options=None):
        pipeline = self.load_pipeline(pipeline_name)
        execution_context = ExecutionContext(
            pipeline=pipeline,
            inputs=inputs,
            options=options or {},
            session_id=self.generate_session_id()
        )
        
        return self.pipeline_executor.run(execution_context)
        
    def schedule_pipeline(self, pipeline_name, schedule, inputs):
        scheduler = self.get_scheduler()
        return scheduler.schedule(
            pipeline=pipeline_name,
            schedule=schedule,
            inputs=inputs,
            callback=self.execute_pipeline
        )

frontend:

  state_management: Redux Toolkit
  ui_library: Material-UI
  charts: [Chart.js, D3.js, Vis.js]
  real_time: WebSocket
  build_tool: Vite
  
backend:
  framework: FastAPI
  language: Python 3.10+
  database: PostgreSQL + Neo4j
  search: Elasticsearch
  cache: Redis
  authentication: JWT + OAuth2
  documentation: Swagger/OpenAPI

      setExecutionStatus('failed');

Results Visualization Interface
typescript
interface ResultsVisualizationProps {
  results: InvestigationResult;
  visualizationType: 'graph' | 'table' | 'timeline' | 'map';
}

const ResultsVisualization: React.FC<ResultsVisualizationProps> = ({ results, visualizationType }) => {
  const renderVisualization = () => {
    switch (visualizationType) {
      case 'graph':
        return <GraphVisualization data={results.graphData} />;
      case 'table':
        return <ResultsTable data={results.tabularData} />;
      case 'timeline':
        return <TimelineVisualization events={results.timelineEvents} />;
      case 'map':
        return <MapVisualization locations={results.locationData} />;
      default:
        return <div>Unknown visualization type</div>;
    }
  };
  
  return (
    <Container>
      <Grid container spacing={3}>
        <Grid item xs={12}>
          <Paper>
            <Typography variant="h6">Investigation Results</Typography>
            <VisualizationControls 
              type={visualizationType}
              onTypeChange={setVisualizationType}
            />
            {renderVisualization()}
          </Paper>
        </Grid>
      </Grid>
    </Container>
  );
};
Tool Management Interface
typescript
interface ToolManagementProps {
  tools: Tool[];
  categories: ToolCategory[];
}

const ToolManagement: React.FC<ToolManagementProps> = ({ tools, categories }) => {
  const [selectedCategory, setSelectedCategory] = useState<string>('all');
  const [toolStatus, setToolStatus] = useState<Record<string, ToolStatus>>({});
  
  const installTool = async (toolName: string) => {
    try {
      await api.installTool(toolName);
      setToolStatus(prev => ({ ...prev, [toolName]: 'installed' }));
    } catch (error) {
      setToolStatus(prev => ({ ...prev, [toolName]: 'failed' }));
    }
  };
  
  const filteredTools = selectedCategory === 'all' 
    ? tools 
    : tools.filter(tool => tool.category === selectedCategory);
  
  return (
    <Container>
      <Grid container spacing={3}>
        <Grid item xs={12} md={3}>
          <Paper>
            <Typography variant="h6">Categories</Typography>
            <CategoryList 
              categories={categories}
              selected={selectedCategory}
              onSelect={setSelectedCategory}
            />
          </Paper>
        </Grid>
        <Grid item xs={12} md={9}>
          <Paper>
            <Typography variant="h6">Tools</Typography>
            <ToolGrid 
              tools={filteredTools}
              status={toolStatus}
              onInstall={installTool}
            />
          </Paper>
        </Grid>
      </Grid>
    </Container>
  );
};

interface ConfigurationProps {

const Configuration: React.FC<ConfigurationProps> = ({ config, onSave }) => {
  const [editedConfig, setEditedConfig] = useState<SystemConfig>(config);
  
  const saveConfiguration = async () => {
    try {
      await api.saveConfiguration(editedConfig);
      onSave(editedConfig);
    } catch (error) {
      // Handle error
    }
  };
  
  return (
    <Container>
      <Paper>
        <Typography variant="h6">System Configuration</Typography>
        <Tabs value={activeTab} onChange={setActiveTab}>
          <Tab label="Database" />
          <Tab label="Security" />
          <Tab label="Monitoring" />
          <Tab label="API Keys" />
        </Tabs>
        
        <TabPanel value={activeTab} index={0}>
          <DatabaseConfig 
            config={editedConfig.database}
            onChange={updateDatabaseConfig}
          />
        </TabPanel>
        
        <TabPanel value={activeTab} index={1}>
          <SecurityConfig 
            config={editedConfig.security}
            onChange={updateSecurityConfig}
          />
        </TabPanel>
        
        <Button 
          onClick={saveConfiguration}
          variant="contained"
          color="primary"
        >
          Save Configuration
        </Button>
      </Paper>
    </Container>
  );

SECTION 6: BACKEND API ARCHITECTURE
API Endpoints Structure
python
# FastAPI Application Structure
from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI(title="R4N3R OSINT Framework API", version="2.0")
security = HTTPBearer()

# Pydantic Models
class PipelineRequest(BaseModel):
    name: str
    inputs: Dict[str, str]
    options: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None

class PipelineResponse(BaseModel):
    session_id: str
    status: str
    results: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    error: Optional[str] = None

# API Routes
@app.post("/api/v1/pipelines/execute", response_model=PipelineResponse)
async def execute_pipeline(
    request: PipelineRequest,
    token: str = Depends(security)
):
    """Execute a specific pipeline"""
    try:
        orchestrator = get_orchestrator()
        result = await orchestrator.execute_pipeline(
            pipeline_name=request.name,
            inputs=request.inputs,
            options=request.options
        )
        return PipelineResponse(
            session_id=result.session_id,
            status="completed",
            results=result.data
        )
    except Exception as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))

@app.get("/api/v1/pipelines")
async def list_pipelines(token: str = Depends(security)):
    """List all available pipelines"""
    return await get_pipeline_registry().list_pipelines()

@app.get("/api/v1/tools")
async def list_tools(token: str = Depends(security)):
    """List all available tools"""
    return await get_tool_registry().list_tools()

@app.post("/api/v1/tools/{tool_name}/install")
async def install_tool(tool_name: str, token: str = Depends(security)):
    """Install a specific tool"""
    return await get_tool_manager().install_tool(tool_name)
Database Models
python
# SQLAlchemy Models
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, DateTime, JSON, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

Base = declarative_base()

class Investigation(Base):
    __tablename__ = "investigations"
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255))
    description = Column(Text)
    created_at = Column(DateTime)
    updated_at = Column(DateTime)
    status = Column(String(50))
    
    results = relationship("InvestigationResult", back_populates="investigation")
    sessions = relationship("ExecutionSession", back_populates="investigation")

class ExecutionSession(Base):
    __tablename__ = "execution_sessions"
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    investigation_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("investigations.id"))
    pipeline_name = Column(String(255))
    inputs = Column(JSON)
    status = Column(String(50))
    started_at = Column(DateTime)
    completed_at = Column(DateTime)
    
    investigation = relationship("Investigation", back_populates="sessions")
    results = relationship("SessionResult", back_populates="session")

class SessionResult(Base):
    __tablename__ = "session_results"
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    session_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("execution_sessions.id"))
    stage_name = Column(String(255))
    tool_name = Column(String(255))
    result_data = Column(JSON)
    created_at = Column(DateTime)
    
    session = relationship("ExecutionSession", back_populates="results")

Nginx Configuration
nginx
events {
    worker_connections 1024;
}

http {
    upstream backend {
        server app:8000;
    }

    upstream frontend {
        server frontend:3000;
    }

    server {
        listen 80;
        server_name localhost;

        # Frontend
        location / {
            proxy_pass http://frontend;
            proxy_set_header Host $host;
            proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        }

        # Backend API
        location /api/ {
            proxy_pass http://backend;
            proxy_set_header Host $host;
            proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        }

        # WebSocket support
        location /ws/ {
            proxy_pass http://backend;
            proxy_http_version 1.1;
            proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
            proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
            proxy_set_header Host $host;
            proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        }
    }
}
SECTION 8: SECURITY CONFIGURATION
Authentication & Authorization
python
# JWT Authentication
from jose import JWTError, jwt
from passlib.context import CryptContext
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

SECRET_KEY = "your-secret-key"
ALGORITHM = "HS256"
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 30

pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")

class SecurityManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.secret_key = SECRET_KEY
        self.algorithm = ALGORITHM
        self.access_token_expire_minutes = ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES
    
    def verify_password(self, plain_password: str, hashed_password: str) -> bool:
        return pwd_context.verify(plain_password, hashed_password)
    
    def get_password_hash(self, password: str) -> str:
        return pwd_context.hash(password)
    
    def create_access_token(self, data: dict, expires_delta: Optional[timedelta] = None):
        to_encode = data.copy()
        if expires_delta:
            expire = datetime.utcnow() + expires_delta
        else:
            expire = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=self.access_token_expire_minutes)
        
        to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
        encoded_jwt = jwt.encode(to_encode, self.secret_key, algorithm=self.algorithm)
        return encoded_jwt
    
    def verify_token(self, token: str) -> Optional[str]:
        try:
            payload = jwt.decode(token, self.secret_key, algorithms=[self.algorithm])
            username: str = payload.get("sub")
            if username is None:
                return None
            return username
        except JWTError:
            return None
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
python
# RBAC Implementation
from enum import Enum
from typing import List, Dict, Set

class Role(Enum):
    ADMIN = "admin"
    ANALYST = "analyst"
    VIEWER = "viewer"
    OPERATOR = "operator"

class Permission(Enum):
    READ_INVESTIGATIONS = "read_investigations"
    WRITE_INVESTIGATIONS = "write_investigations"
    EXECUTE_PIPELINES = "execute_pipelines"
    MANAGE_TOOLS = "manage_tools"
    MANAGE_USERS = "manage_users"
    VIEW_SYSTEM_METRICS = "view_system_metrics"
    CONFIGURE_SYSTEM = "configure_system"

# Role-Permission Mapping
ROLE_PERMISSIONS: Dict[Role, Set[Permission]] = {
    Role.ADMIN: {
        Permission.READ_INVESTIGATIONS,
        Permission.WRITE_INVESTIGATIONS,
        Permission.EXECUTE_PIPELINES,
        Permission.MANAGE_TOOLS,
        Permission.MANAGE_USERS,
        Permission.VIEW_SYSTEM_METRICS,
        Permission.CONFIGURE_SYSTEM
    },
    Role.ANALYST: {
        Permission.READ_INVESTIGATIONS,
        Permission.WRITE_INVESTIGATIONS,
        Permission.EXECUTE_PIPELINES,
        Permission.VIEW_SYSTEM_METRICS
    },
    Role.OPERATOR: {
        Permission.EXECUTE_PIPELINES,
        Permission.READ_INVESTIGATIONS
    },
    Role.VIEWER: {
        Permission.READ_INVESTIGATIONS,
        Permission.VIEW_SYSTEM_METRICS
    }
}

class AuthorizationManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.role_permissions = ROLE_PERMISSIONS
    
    def has_permission(self, user_role: Role, permission: Permission) -> bool:
        return permission in self.role_permissions.get(user_role, set())
    
    def check_permissions(self, user_role: Role, required_permissions: List[Permission]) -> bool:
        return all(self.has_permission(user_role, perm) for perm in required_permissions)
API Security Middleware
python
# Security Middleware
from fastapi import Request, HTTPException
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

class SecurityMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    def __init__(self, app, security_manager: SecurityManager):
        super().__init__(app)
        self.security_manager = security_manager
    
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        # Rate limiting
        await self.check_rate_limit(request)
        
        # Input validation
        await self.validate_input(request)
        
        # Security headers
        response = await call_next(request)
        response.headers["X-Content-Type-Options"] = "nosniff"
        response.headers["X-Frame-Options"] = "DENY"
        response.headers["X-XSS-Protection"] = "1; mode=block"
        response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"
        
        return response
    
    async def check_rate_limit(self, request: Request):
        # Implement rate limiting logic
        pass
    
    async def validate_input(self, request: Request):
        # Implement input validation logic
        pass

Logging Configuration
python
# Structured Logging
import logging
import json
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, Any

class StructuredLogger:
    def __init__(self, name: str):
        self.logger = logging.getLogger(name)
        self.logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
        
        # Create formatter
        formatter = logging.Formatter(
            '%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s'
        )
        
        # Create handlers
        console_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
        file_handler = logging.FileHandler('r4nger.log')
        
        console_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
        
        self.logger.addHandler(console_handler)
        self.logger.addHandler(file_handler)
    
    def log_pipeline_execution(self, pipeline_name: str, session_id: str, 
                             status: str, duration: float, error: str = None):
        log_data = {
            'event_type': 'pipeline_execution',
            'pipeline_name': pipeline_name,
            'session_id': session_id,
            'status': status,
            'duration': duration,
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'error': error
        }
        
        if status == 'completed':
            self.logger.info(json.dumps(log_data))
        elif status == 'failed':
            self.logger.error(json.dumps(log_data))
        else:
            self.logger.warning(json.dumps(log_data))
    
    def log_tool_execution(self, tool_name: str, stage: str, 
                          status: str, result_data: Dict[str, Any]):
        log_data = {
            'event_type': 'tool_execution',
            'tool_name': tool_name,
            'stage': stage,
            'status': status,
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'result_count': len(result_data) if result_data else 0
        }
        
        self.logger.info(json.dumps(log_data))
    
    def log_security_event(self, event_type: str, user: str, 
                          details: Dict[str, Any]):
        log_data = {
            'event_type': 'security_event',
            'security_event_type': event_type,
            'user': user,
            'details': details,
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        }
        
        self.logger.warning(json.dumps(log_data))

  group_wait: 10s
  group_interval: 10s
  repeat_interval: 1h
  receiver: 'web.hook'

- name: 'web.hook'
  email_configs:
  - to: 'admin@r4nger.local'
    subject: '[R4N3R] Alert: {{ .GroupLabels.alertname }}'
    body: |
      {{ range .Alerts }}
      Alert: {{ .Annotations.summary }}
      Description: {{ .Annotations.description }}
      {{ end }}

    channel: '#r4nger-alerts'
    title: 'R4N3R Alert'
    text: '{{ range .Alerts }}{{ .Annotations.summary }}{{ end }}'

    severity: 'critical'
  target_match:
    severity: 'warning'
