SECTION 10: TESTING FRAMEWORK
Unit Tests
python
# Unit Tests for Pipeline Execution
import pytest
from unittest.mock import Mock, patch
from r4nger.pipeline_executor import PipelineExecutor
from r4nger.models import ExecutionContext, Pipeline

class TestPipelineExecutor:
    def setup_method(self):
        self.executor = PipelineExecutor()
        self.mock_pipeline = Pipeline(
            name="test_pipeline",
            stages=[],
            input_schema={"email": "string"}
        )
    
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_execute_pipeline_success(self):
        # Arrange
        context = ExecutionContext(
            pipeline=self.mock_pipeline,
            inputs={"email": "test@example.com"},
            options={},
            session_id="test_session"
        )
        
        # Act
        result = await self.executor.run(context)
        
        # Assert
        assert result.status == "completed"
        assert result.session_id == "test_session"
    
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_execute_pipeline_with_invalid_input(self):
        # Arrange
        context = ExecutionContext(
            pipeline=self.mock_pipeline,
            inputs={},  # Missing required email
            options={},
            session_id="test_session"
        )
        
        # Act & Assert
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Missing required input"):
            await self.executor.run(context)
    
    @patch('r4nger.tools.tool_registry.ToolRegistry.get_tool')
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_execute_stage_with_tool_failure(self, mock_get_tool):
        # Arrange
        mock_tool = Mock()
        mock_tool.execute.side_effect = Exception("Tool execution failed")
        mock_get_tool.return_value = mock_tool
        
        stage = Mock()
        stage.tools = ["test_tool"]
        stage.module = "test_module"
        
        # Act & Assert
        with pytest.raises(Exception, match="Tool execution failed"):
            await self.executor._execute_stage(stage, {}, "test_session")
Integration Tests
python
# Integration Tests
import pytest
import asyncio
from testcontainers.postgres import PostgreSqlContainer
from testcontainers.neo4j import Neo4jContainer
from r4nger.orchestrator import WorkflowOrchestrator

class TestIntegration:
    @pytest.fixture(scope="class")
    def setup_containers(self):
        # Start test containers
        postgres = PostgreSqlContainer("postgres:14")
        neo4j = Neo4jContainer("neo4j:4.4")
        
        postgres.start()
        neo4j.start()
        
        yield {
            "postgres_url": postgres.get_connection_url(),
            "neo4j_url": neo4j.get_connection_url()
        }
        
        postgres.stop()
        neo4j.stop()
    
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_full_pipeline_execution(self, setup_containers):
        # Arrange
        orchestrator = WorkflowOrchestrator(config_path="test_config.yaml")
        orchestrator.initialize_databases(setup_containers)
        
        # Act
        result = await orchestrator.execute_pipeline(
            pipeline_name="email_intelligence_pipeline",
            inputs={"email": "test@example.com"}
        )
        
        # Assert
        assert result.status == "completed"
        assert "validation_results" in result.data
        assert "social_profiles" in result.data
    
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ai_correlation_engine(self, setup_containers):
        # Arrange
        correlation_engine = AICorrelationEngine()
        test_data = {
            "emails": ["test@example.com"],
            "social_profiles": [{"platform": "twitter", "username": "testuser"}],
            "domains": ["example.com"]
        }
        
        # Act
        correlations = await correlation_engine.find_correlations(test_data)
        
        # Assert
        assert len(correlations) > 0
        assert any(correlation.confidence > 0.8 for correlation in correlations)
Performance Tests
python
# Performance Tests
import pytest
import asyncio
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

class TestPerformance:
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_pipeline_execution_performance(self):
        # Arrange
        executor = PipelineExecutor()
        pipeline = load_test_pipeline()
        
        # Act
        start_time = time.time()
        tasks = []
        
        # Execute 10 pipelines concurrently
        for i in range(10):
            task = executor.run(create_test_context(pipeline, f"test{i}@example.com"))
            tasks.append(task)
        
        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
        end_time = time.time()
        
        # Assert
        execution_time = end_time - start_time
        assert execution_time < 60  # Should complete within 60 seconds
        assert all(result.status == "completed" for result in results)
    
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_database_query_performance(self, setup_containers):
        # Arrange
        db = DatabaseConnection(setup_containers["postgres_url"])
        
        # Act
        start_time = time.time()
        
        # Execute 100 concurrent queries
        tasks = []
        for i in range(100):
            task = db.execute_query("SELECT * FROM investigations WHERE id = %s", (i,))
            tasks.append(task)
        
        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
        end_time = time.time()
        
        # Assert
        query_time = end_time - start_time
        assert query_time < 10  # Should complete within 10 seconds
        assert len(results) == 100
